title: See your account details in settings
summary: Replaces the empty settings screen with a real account summary that shows people their username, email, sign-in method, note count, and join date. It gives the settings area a working foundation that later settings features can build on.
motivation: Replace the settings placeholder with a backend-backed account summary and establish the settings feature seam.

included: GET /api/settings/user-info remains the backend account-summary contract
a minimal backend settings service module extracts user-info business logic out of the router
a typed frontend settings API client and composable fetch user info
the AppShell settings placeholder is replaced with a SettingsView account summary
tests cover local and OIDC user-info rendering, loading, error, and retry states
excluded: password changes, note export, and account deletion UI; those are separate settings binders
OIDC browser sign-in flow; this binder only renders account metadata for users who are already authenticated
new routing, Pinia, Vue Router, or third-party UI dependencies
new design tokens or hand edits to generated ui/src/design-system/tokens.css

design_facts.stack: Mixed Vue 3/TypeScript/Vite frontend plus FastAPI/Python 3.13/SQLAlchemy async/PostgreSQL backend; DTCG design tokens; no router or Pinia.

token_manifest.system: W3C DTCG JSON built with Style Dictionary into CSS custom properties.
token_manifest.consumption_rule: New component CSS consumes semantic custom properties only; primitives are not used in new code.

work_items.title: Gather a person's account details
work_items.summary: Moves the work of gathering a person's account details out of the web route and into a dedicated service so the route stays simple.

exposes.function: async get_user_info(db: AsyncSession, current_user: User) -> UserInfoResponse

behavior: queries Note with Note.user_id == current_user.id in the same statement
returns username, email, created_at ISO string, notes_count, and auth_provider through UserInfoResponse
keeps routers/settings.py declarative: dependency wiring plus one service call
uses module-level functions, not a class or factory with one implementation

signals: contract-mutation:additive backend service function consumed by settings router
sensitive-zone:settings route uses auth and tenant-scoped note counts
blast-radius:router business logic moves into services

oracle.command: make test-backend-lint && make test-backend-format && make test-backend-types && cd backend && uv run pytest tests/integration/test_settings.py::TestGetUserInfo tests/unit/test_settings_schemas.py::TestUserInfoResponse -v
assertions: GET /api/settings/user-info still returns the same UserInfoResponse shape for local and OIDC users.
The note count query remains tenant-scoped with Note.user_id == current_user.id.
routers/settings.py delegates user-info assembly to backend/app/services/settings_service.py.

work_items.title: Show your account details in settings
work_items.summary: Builds the settings screen people actually see, which loads their account facts and shows clear loading, error, and retry states.

component_map.notes: Container component owns settings fetch state and renders compact account facts.
Use native buttons for retry; no new design-system abstraction.

behavior: SettingsView fetches account info when opened and shows username, email when present, note count, auth provider, and account creation date
loading and error states are visible and retryable
AppShell keeps the existing no-router settingsActive toggle and renders SettingsView instead of the placeholder
all state uses Vue refs/composables; no Pinia, router, or localStorage settings cache is introduced

signals: blast-radius:AppShell settings pane changes from placeholder to fetched view

oracle.command: make test-ui-lint && cd ui && npm run test -- src/services/settings.test.ts src/features/settings/useSettings.test.ts src/features/settings/SettingsView.test.ts src/features/shell/__tests__/AppShell.test.ts
assertions: Opening settings calls GET /api/settings/user-info with the bearer token and renders the returned account facts.
Loading, API-error, and retry states are covered without mutating AppShell navigation state.
The settings view uses typed props/emits or composable returns, stable v-for keys where lists are rendered, and semantic CSS variables only.
